blocks.content:
A data source variable populates a dropdown with all configured instances of a specific data source type. When a user selects an instance from the dropdown, every panel that references the variable switches to query that instance.

To create a data source variable, complete the following steps:
Open an existing dashboard, or create a new one by clicking **New** > **New Dashboard**.
Click the **Edit** toggle in the top toolbar to enter edit mode.
Click the **Dashboard settings** gear icon in the toolbar.

In the settings sidebar, click **Variables**.

The Variables section opens, showing any existing variables or an empty state.

blocks.reftarget: data-testid Call to action button Add variable
blocks.content:
Click **Add variable**.

The variable editor opens with a form for configuring the new variable.

blocks.reftarget: data-testid Variable editor Form Type select
blocks.content:
In the **Type** dropdown, select **Data source**.

The form updates to show data source-specific options.

In the **Type** field under the Data source options, select the data source type you want (for example, **TestData**).

The **Preview of values** section updates to show all configured instances of that type.
Click **Back to list** to save the variable and return to the variables list.
A dropdown now appears at the top of the dashboard with all instances of the selected data source type. In the next milestone, you use this variable in a panel query.
